title: aeluropus littoralis var. dasyphyllus
intro: Aeluropus littoralis var. dasyphyllus, also known as sea couch grass, is a type of perennial grass that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of sea couch grass include its ability to soothe irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, sea couch grass is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical creams, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
